title: Hungary blocks EU Ukraine loan, Russia sanctions on eve of war anniversary
description: Hungary maintained its veto on Monday against a new EU package of sanctions against Russia and a €90 billion loan to Ukraine, while a Ukrainian strike against a Russian pumping station serving the Druzhba oil pipeline threatened to further worsen relations between Kyiv and Budapest.

description: Peter Mandelson was arrested on Monday on suspicion of “misconduct in public office” following revelations about his dealings with Jeffrey Epstein, the sex offender.

title: Britain's former US ambassador Peter Mandelson arrested amid Epstein revelations
description: London police arrested former UK ambassador to the US Peter Mandelson on Monday as part of a misconduct probe over his ties to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Emails released by the US Department of Justice in January appeared to show Mandelson sharing information with the disgraced financier while a minister in then-premier Gordon Brown's cabinet.

title: Blizzard warnings, travel bans in effect as brutal storm pounds Northeast

A senior U.S. official told Axios on Sunday morning the Trump administration expects to receive the proposal by Tuesday. title: Faith leaders accuse White House Faith Office of stonewalling them
description: Some faith leaders say they're being shut out by the White House Faith Office, alleging the administration provides access only to those in political lockstep while ignoring the impact of ICE raids on religious attendance.Why it matters: The office is taxpayer-funded and ostensibly represents the full breadth of American religious life. Critics argue it has instead become a political arm for conservative Christians.Catch up quick: President Trump

title: Lindsey Graham frets that Trump's advisers oppose Iran strike
description: Senator Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) told Axios on Saturday that several people around President Trump are advising him not to bomb Iran. title: Russian MOD Shows U.S. F-35, F-16 Intercepting Tu-95MS And Su-35S Off Alaska
description: The intercept was carried out as the Russian Tu-95MS bombers and their escorting fighters transited the Alaskan ADIZ (Air Defense Identification Zone). On Feb. 19, 2026, the U.S. Air Force launched two F-16s, two F-35s, one E-3, and four KC-135s tankers to intercept, positively identify, and escort the aircraft until they departed the Alaskan ADIZ. []

title: Ukrainian Air Force F-16 Captured Shooting Down Russian Target with APKWS II

title: The revamp of Ireland’s defence
description:
Despite a long-standing policy of neutrality, Ireland has recognised Russia as a threat, and is following Europe in rearmament.

title: Sweden orders eight Seasnake 30 systems from Rheinmetall
description:
Sweden has contracted German defence company Rheinmetall to provide eight Seasnake 30 weapon systems for use by the Swedish Navy.
The post Sweden orders eight Seasnake 30 systems from Rheinmetall appeared first on Naval Technology.

title: Israel strikes Hamas in Lebanon, Palestinian terrorists violate Gaza ceasefire 14 times from February 5 to 20, IDF says
description:
As US President Donald Trump convened the Gaza Board of Peace for its inaugural meeting in Washington, DC, the IDF continued to report numerous ceasefire violations in the territory. Israel also struck the Ain al Hilweh Palestinian refugee camp near Sidon, Lebanon, stating that the target was used for the “preparation of terrorist activities against IDF forces in Lebanese territory.”
The post Israel strikes Hamas in Lebanon, Palestinian terrorist

title: Stabilization force and funding pledged for Gaza at Board of Peace meeting
description: At a February 19 meeting of the US-backed Board of Peace in Washington, DC, five countries pledged troops to an International Stabilization Force for Gaza, and representatives of 40 nations committed approximately $7 billion in funding. The initiative is part of the second phase of a ceasefire plan launched in October 2025. Clashes between the Israel Defense Forces and militants continue in Gaza, where Israeli forces report ongoing ceasefire viol

title: Israeli operations in Lebanon against Hezbollah: February 9–15, 2026
description:
Israel continued military operations in Lebanon between February 9 and February 15, 2026. Israeli activities last week were concentrated in south Lebanon and consisted largely of policing activity, maintaining the reduced intensity of previous weeks.
The post Israeli operations in Lebanon against Hezbollah: February 9–15, 2026 appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: Syrian government and SDF continue transition, US strikes Islamic State and withdraws from Tanf
description: The Syrian government and the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) have continued implementing a January transition agreement. US Central Command has maintained pressure on the Islamic State with airstrikes and withdrew from the Tanf garrison in southern Syria. Damascus is assuming control of Islamic State detention sites in the eastern part of the country. Senior US, Syrian, and SDF officials met in Munich to discuss integration, the ceasefire impleme

title: Hamas renews threats against rival Palestinian militias in Gaza
description:
Hamas warned opposing militias that their days were numbered and that the Israeli military could not protect them. The threat came after some militias have carried out successful operations against Hamas in recent weeks and months. 
The post Hamas renews threats against rival Palestinian militias in Gaza appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: Israeli operations in Lebanon against Hezbollah: February 2–8, 2026
description:
Israel continued military operations in Lebanon between February 2 and February 8, 2026. Israeli activities last week were concentrated in south Lebanon and significantly less intense and numerous than in preceding weeks.
The post Israeli operations in Lebanon against Hezbollah: February 2–8, 2026 appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: Turkish foreign minister warns of nuclear arms race if Iran gets the bomb
description: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan warned on February 9 that Ankara may pursue nuclear weapons if Iran acquires them, signaling a potential shift from rhetorical grievance to a conditional proliferation policy. While downplaying evidence of Iran’s weaponization and opposing US military strikes, Turkish officials continue to legitimize Tehran’s nuclear activities while framing Turkish nuclearization as a defensive necessity tied to regional “bal

title: Generation Jihad | The West’s greatest threat is still Al Qaeda
description: In its latest report on the state of global jihad, the UN reveals that Al Qaeda is expanding but doesn't mention one critical fact: the jihadist group's leader is based in Iran. Bill Roggio is joined by his FDD colleague Edmund Fitton-Brown—who previously oversaw the UN’s sanctions and monitoring team that produces these assessments—to unpack what the report gets right, what it misses, and why Al Qaeda—not the Islamic State—remains the most dange

title: Syria joins anti-Islamic State coalition meeting as territory transition with SDF continues
description: Syria’s foreign minister joined a US-led anti-Islamic State coalition meeting in Riyadh on February 9, signaling deeper coordination after Damascus joined the coalition in November. The meeting followed a January 29 agreement between the Syrian government and the Syrian Democratic Forces enabling government deployments into northeastern Syria. Coalition members praised the deal and discussed transfers of Islamic State detainees to Iraq. As Syrian

title: Analysis: Naim Qassem delivers defiant speech to mark anniversary of Mahdi Schools
description:
Naim Qassem marked the 33rd anniversary of the Mahdi Schools System with a defiant speech that said Iran was divinely promised a momentous victory over the United States and Israel. He also stressed that Hezbollah would not disarm, and its patient path of choosing even death over surrender would guarantee Lebanon's eventual victory over Israel.
The post Analysis: Naim Qassem delivers defiant speech to mark anniversary of Mahdi Schools appeared fi

title: After Damascus-SDF deal, Syrian security forces deploy in northeastern Syria
description: Syrian government forces deployed into Al Hasakah and Qamishli in eastern Syria in early February under a January 30 agreement with the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF). The deal is aimed at ending clashes and restoring central authority in mainly Kurdish areas that the SDF still controls. The government deployment coincided with reports of continued US transfers of Islamic State detainees from Syria to Iraq and US strikes on Islamic State targets.

title: Terror concerns as Ukraine-Russia war hits four-year mark
description: An attack in the western city of Lviv has raised concerns over the prospect of a growing terror threat in Ukrainian cities.

title: UK joins European air defence project despite DIP hold-up
description: The UK has joined a pan-European air defence programme known as LEAP, intended to detect and intercept hostile small drones.

title: New Army unit seeks to disrupt ‘malign influence’ in Indo-Pacific
description: The 1st Theater Information Advantage Detachment in Hawaii is the first of three such units to activate, with two units planned for Georgia and Germany.

title: Top US admiral says he’s watching China’s rapid naval buildup closely
description: Adm. Daryl Caudle has called China’s shipbuilding capability “impressive” as Beijing moves ahead with a rapid buildup of the world’s largest navy.

title: US and Russia agree to reestablish military dialogue after Ukraine talks
description: High-level military communication was suspended in late 2021, as tension between Moscow and Washington rose ahead of the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

description: The Pentagon declared on Friday that the U.S. military’s top priority is to defend the homeland and Western Hemisphere.

title: How the US Army secretary became a key figure in Ukraine peace talks
description: While Driscoll’s role as Army secretary typically involves bureaucratic tasks, he’s also taken on the challenge of diplomacy.

title: Proposed peace plan for Ukraine leaves country in delicate position
description: A U.S. proposal to end the war in Ukraine puts the country in a delicate position — caught between placating the U.S. and not capitulating to Russia.

title: Sweden 'very happy to be members of NATO', Swedish ambassador to France says
description: As the world marks the fourth anniversary of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the Swedish ambassador to France has spoken of her country's commitment to the Ukrainian cause. It is now almost two years since Sweden joined NATO after years of being a nonaligned country. Caroline Vicini says it was a big but necessary step for Sweden. She spoke to us in Perspective.

title: EU lawmakers to freeze US trade deal after Supreme Court strikes down Trump's tariffs
description: The European Parliament will suspend the approval of a key trade agreement between the EU and the US on Monday, pending clarification from Washington on the impact of the US Supreme Court's decision last week invalidating many of President Donald Trump's tariffs.

title: Jamie Woon and Blackpink: Musical comebacks of 2026
description: In this edition of our arts24 music show, Jennifer Ben Brahim chats with British artist Jamie Woon, who is back with new music for the first time in a decade. He emerged from London's fertile post-dubstep scene of the early 2010s with his debut album "Mirrorwriting" and his Mercury-nominated follow-up "Making Time". His lyrics are vulnerable and introspective, blending R'n'B, neo soul and electronic sounds. After a decade away from the spotlight,

title: US trade deals thrown into doubt after Trump's new tariffs
description: The European ⁠Parliament decided on ​Monday to postpone a vote on the European ​Union's trade deal with the United States due to U.S. President Donald Trump's imposition of a blanket 15% import duty after ​the Supreme ‌Court struck down his previous ⁠global tariffs. Other countries too are re-examining deals with the US in light of the ruling.

title: 'Nervous times in Tehran' ahead of crunch US talks and strike threat
description: Iran warned the United States on Monday that any attack would be met "ferociously" in response to President Donald Trump's threat of limited strikes, while students staged new protests against the Islamic republic. Tehran and Washington's threats came as both sides worked to reach a deal in indirect talks due to restart in Switzerland on Thursday. France 24's Reza Sayah has the latest from Tehran.

title: Ukraine: Europe on the front line
description: Four years after Russia's full-scale invasion, the EU has established itself as Ukraine's main source of support. With a loan of €90 billion voted through in February 2026, the EU has confirmed its massive commitment to Kyiv, while the Trump administration has pulled back.

title: France to summon US ambassador over comments on far-right activist's killing
description: France will summon the US ambassador to Paris over comments made by the Trump administration about the killing of a French far-right activist blamed on the hard left, the foreign minister said Sunday. Commenting on the death of Quentin Deranque, the US State Department's bureau of counter-terrorism posted on X that ”violent radical leftism is on the rise” and poses a threat to public safety.

title: New York paralysed by a historic snowstorm
description:
The storm is affecting more than 40 million people in the north-east, from Maryland to Maine.
 
Several states have declared a state of emergency and imposed travel bans during specific time periods.

title: Hungary vows to block EU cash for Ukraine, Russia sanctions
description: Hungary looked set on Monday to carry out a threat to block new EU ​sanctions on Moscow and a huge loan for Kyiv, in a blow to Europe's pro-Ukrainian consensus on the eve of the war's fourth anniversary.

title: Iran: Students rally one month after deadly protests
description:
Students across Iran have launched anti-government protests, the largest demonstrations of this kind since the authorities’ deadly crackdown in January.
 
At the same time, Iran is facing a US military build-up intended to increase pressure for a nuclear agreement.

title: Winter storm hits US Northeast, shuts down New York City
description: A winter storm has shut down New York City and much of the US Northeast, bringing heavy snowfall and strong winds.

description: Mike Huckabee suggested Israel would be justified in taking much of the Middle East on Biblical grounds.

description: The 27-nation bloc isn’t throwing out the deal it struck with the United States, but officials want more clarity before they implement it.

description: Ukraine's president sat down with the BBC's Jeremy Bowen in Kyiv days before the four-year anniversary of the war.

description: The suspect was carrying a shotgun and fuel can when he was killed, officers say, while Trump was in Washington DC at the time.

description: The Kremlin’s sabotage campaign against European allies of Ukraine has been escalating. It needs people like Aleksei Kolosovsky, 42, to carry it out.

description: Student groups reported protests at universities in Iran’s two largest cities as the government grapples with domestic discontent and the threat of U.S. strikes.

description: The ambassador, Mike Huckabee, seemed to endorse Israeli control of lands stretching from Egypt to Iraq. He said his comments were taken out of context.

description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has entrusted Ali Larijani, the top national security official, to ensure the Islamic Republic endures any military attacks and targeted killings.

description: The negotiations in Switzerland were the third round of direct talks organized by the U.S. Expectations for significant progress in Geneva were low.

title: US to send more missile launchers to the Philippines despite China’s alarm
description: The addition of missile launchers comes as treaty allies condemned what they called China’s “illegal, coercive, aggressive, and deceptive activities.”

title: Airbus open to two-fighter option for FCAS to keep program alive
description: “The deadlock of a single pillar should not jeopardize the entire future of this high-tech European capability, which will bolster our collective defense."

title: Robert Mugabe's son charged with attempted murder in South Africa
description: Bellarmine Mugabe and one other man have not commented on the charges after a 23-year-old was shot.

title: In Brussels, volunteers assemble drones to be sent to Ukraine
description: In undisclosed locations, volunteers meet regularly in the Belgian capital to assemble drones destined for Ukraine. Teachers, European civil servants, members of the Ukrainian diaspora and former military personnel are participating in this initiative coordinated by the NGO Wings for Europe. Funded by private donations, these devices are then sent to the front lines. FRANCE 24's Alix Le Bourdon and Dave Keating report from Brussels.

title: Why are israel and india getting closer?
description: India’s Prime Minister Narendra Modi is heading to Israel, where he’s set to meet Benjamin Netanyahu.

title: BAE says its Eurofighter pipeline is filled until first GCAP assembly
description: Europe accounted for 32% of the BAE order book at the end of December, while accounting for 11% of sales in 2025, the company said.

title: Pentagon official blesses Europe’s push to spend defense money at home
description: “Otherwise people in Germany or Poland or whatever are going to say: ‘Why are we only sending money across the Atlantic?’” said the Pentagon policy chief.

title: Pentagon to deploy roughly 200 troops to Nigeria
description: The 200 troops will supplement a small team of American military officers already embedded with Nigerian forces.

title: Experts worry about nuclear quid pro quo in Russia-North Korea alliance against Ukraine
description: Analysts differ on the question of whether Moscow would trade away highly sensitive nuclear submarine technology in exchange for battlefield support.

title: Estonia begins buying hundreds of pop-up bunkers to fortify border with Russia
description: The Baltic nations are installing a variety of border security features meant to repel an invasion from Russia or Belarus.

title: ‘You either fight or die’: Kenyans tricked into joining Russia-Ukraine war
description: More than 1,000 Kenyans and people from 36 African nations are fighting for Russia, many of them recruited fraudulently.

title: In Pakistan, female boxers break down gender barriers to step into the ring
description: In Pakistan, more and more women are stepping into the ring — a space long seen as the preserve of men. At the 5th National Women’s Boxing Championship, participation surpassed 100 athletes for the first time, competing across eight weight divisions — compared to just three at the tournament’s inaugural edition in 2018.

title: More protests in Iran as talks continue with US
description: Students ⁠held protests which led ​to clashes at several Iranian universities for a ​second day on Sunday, according to local news agencies and social media posts, with Iran facing a U.S. military ​buildup ‌as it seeks to reach a ⁠nuclear deal with Washington.

title: New York orders citywide travel ban as major blizzard hits eastern US
description: New York ordered drivers off the road and shut down schools on Monday, while residents braced for a massive snowstorm hitting the United States northeast. Tens of millions of Americans from the US capital, Washington, to the northern state of Maine have prepared for up to two feet (60 centimeters) of snow forecast in some areas.

title: Iran says ready for talks but will defend itself against US aggression
description: Remarks from Ministry of Foreign Affairs come after Trump says he is considering an attack if nuclear deal not reached.

title: Gaza child dies after Israel blocks medical evacuation
description: Palestinian boy Nidal Abu Rabeea died in Gaza after waiting months for Israel to allow his medical evacuation.

title: Gaza child dies waiting for Israeli permission to leave for treatment
description: Israel continues to block thousands of Palestinians from seeking medical treatment abroad through Rafah crossing.

title: Tour Montparnasse Is One of Paris’s Uglier Landmarks. It’s Getting a Makeover.
description: The Tour Montparnasse is one of Paris’s least-loved landmarks. After half a century, it’s finally being remodeled.

title: For Iran’s Rulers, Refusing U.S. Demands Is a Risk Worth Taking
description: The government in Tehran sees capitulating to Washington’s demands on uranium enrichment and ballistic missiles as riskier to its survival than going to war, analysts say.

title: EU Support for Ukraine Stumbles as Hungary Looks to Delay Aid
description: Hungary said that it would block both the latest sanctions package on Russia and a financial aid package to Kyiv worth about $106 billion.

title: The Ukrainian Bureaucrat Working to Squeeze Russia’s War Machine
description: Vladyslav Vlasiuk has spent the past four years pressing Western allies to squeeze the Russian economy through more sanctions. He hopes that 2026 will be the tipping point.

title: Scoop: Hegseth to meet Anthropic CEO as Pentagon threatens banishment
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has summoned Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei to the Pentagon on Tuesday morning for what sources say is likely to be a tense meeting over terms for military use of Anthropic's Claude."Anthropic knows this is not a get-to-know-you meeting," a senior Defense official told Axios. "This is not a friendly meeting. This is a sh*t-or-get-off-the-pot meeting."Why it matters: Claude is the only AI model available in the military'

title: New partnerships enhance Canada’s readiness for future naval projects
description:
Ontario Shipyards and Hanwha Ocean have formalised an MoU aimed at advancing Canada’s capacity to undertake future naval projects.
The post New partnerships enhance Canada’s readiness for future naval projects appeared first on Naval Technology.

title: Massive US Air Force warplane movements in Bulgaria raise stakes for Iran talks
description: The U.S. buildup coincides with high-stakes nuclear diplomacy over Iran's nuclear program.

title: US calls on partners to stick to existing trade deals amid tariff uncertainty
description: After a weekend of trade upheaval in the wake of the US Supreme Court's decision to invalidate US president's tariffs that invoked the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, and Donald Trump's subsequent decision to apply 15 percent global tariffs based on another law, we look at the latest developments in global trade. Also in this edition, robotaxis are vowing to hit the streets of London by the end of 2026.

title: Russia's 'new secret agents' wage a war of sabotage in Europe
description: PRESS REVIEW – Monday, February 23, 2026: First, ahead of the four-year anniversary of the war in Ukraine, papers discuss Russia's war plans, and their new agents. Next, the Guardian takes a closer look at "El Mencho". Also, as the Olympics come to an end, Olympians are celebrated in papers around the world. Finally, the final candidates for European Tree of the year have been named!

title: Gazans face impossible wait at Rafah crossing to Egypt
description: Photos have circulated showing celebrations as formerly exiled Gazans have been able to return home since the partial reopening of the Rafah border crossing between the Gaza Strip and Egypt. However, that isn’t the story for the majority of people seeking to cross: with only a few dozen people allowed through each day, many Gazans are stuck waiting, with no sense of when or if they will be able to cross.

title: 'Love doesn't die with death': How four years of war have reshaped relationships in Ukraine
description: As Ukraine prepares to mark the fourth anniversary of Russia’s brutal invasion, FRANCE 24 spoke to Maryna Kumeda, the Ukrainian author of “Love in Wartime”, about how the war is reshaping attitudes to love and relationships.

title: 'Joy amid the destruction': Gazans decorate rubble-strewn streets for Ramadan
description: Little Ramadan lanterns and string lights have appeared on streets lined with collapsed buildings and piles of rubble in Gaza City, bringing a small moment of joy and respite as Islam's holiest month began - the first since October's ceasefire - gets underway.

title: Iran-US set for last-ditch talks as strike fears build
description: Iranian officials held out hope for progress towards a deal to forestall fresh conflict when talks with US negotiators resume on Thursday, despite a huge build-up of American military might in the Middle East. France 24's Kethevane Gorjestani takes a look at whether an agreement seems likely, and what might happen if one cannot be reached.

title: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani orders citywide shutdown as snowstorm hits
description: The citys entire traffic network is shut down except for emergency travel as a severe snowstorm hits northeast US.

title: Ramadan in Gaza: Cost of iftar doubles as genocidal war devastates economy
description: Al Jazeera analysis shows cost of a basic Ramadan meal in Gaza doubles under Israeli siege.

title: Russia kills four Ukrainians on eve of fourth war anniversary
description: EU foreign ministers are meeting in Brussels to discuss a loan for Kyiv and 20th package of sanctions against Russia.

title: More than 200 Venezuelan political prisoners launch hunger strike over amnesty law exclusions
description: More than 200 Venezuelan political prisoners began a hunger strike on Sunday, demanding inclusion in a new amnesty law they say excludes many detainees, particularly those facing military-related charges. The law, approved by Congress last week, forms part of reforms backed by Washington after former president Nicolas Maduro’s January ouster.

title: Premier League: Arsenal pass derby test of nerve against Tottenham
description: Eberechi Eze and Victor Gyökeres allowed Arsenal to restore their lead at the top of the Premier League table to five points with victory in the North London derby (4-1).

title: Live: Several killed in Ukraine's Odesa as strikes intensify across Ukraine
description: Russian strikes killed three people and wounded several others overnight, regional authorities said Monday, as Moscow intensified its assaults on the eve of the war's four-year anniversary. In the south, two people were killed when Russian drones hit industrial, energy and civilian infrastructure in the Odesa region, regional governor Oleg Kiper said on Telegram.Follow our live blog for all the latest devleopments.

title: Pakistan claims at least 70 fighters killed in strikes along Afghan border
description: Afghan officials accuse Pakistan of targeting civilians and violating its sovereignty in Sundays border air raids.

title: The Ukraine war in numbers: People, territory, money
description: Four years into Russias grinding war on Ukraine, Al Jazeera looks at the costs — to people, territory and treasuries.

title: Four years into its full-scale war in Ukraine, Russia is feeling the effects
description: Steve Rosenberg reports on the economic consequences of Russia's war, and how people are coping.

title: Duterte’s Fans Rally in The Hague as the I.C.C. Weighs Evidence Against Him
description: The protests are a sign of the Philippine ex-leader’s ongoing influence, even as he faces a possible trial for the deaths of thousands during his war on drugs.

title: Mexico killed "El Mencho." Here's how and what we know about U.S. role in the operation
description: Mexican security forces killed Nemesio Rubén Oseguera Cervantes, the country's most-wanted drug lord, following information from the U.S. that assisted in Sunday's operation, the Trump administration and Mexico's defense ministry said.Why it matters: President Trump has for months pressed Mexican officials to target drug cartels and the U.S. offered a reward of up to $15 million for information leading to the arrest of Oseguera Cervantes, known a

title: China calls on US to cancel Trump-era tariffs following Supreme Court ruling
description: China on Monday urged the United States to cancel unilateral tariffs announced by President Donald Trump after the US Supreme Court struck down many of his measures, ruling he lacked authority under a 1977 law. Trump responded by unveiling new global import duties of up to 15% under different powers.

title: Paris court to review Nicolas Sarkozy's request to merge sentences in graft case
description: A Paris criminal court will on Monday review former president Nicolas Sarkozy’s request to combine sentences handed down in separate convictions for corruption and illegal campaign financing. Sarkozy, who served from 2007 to 2012, has faced multiple legal cases since leaving office. His lawyer said such requests are routine.

title: How Modi ‘broke down walls’ between India, Israel – at Palestine’s expense
description: Under Modi, India, a long supporter of the Palestinian cause, has found a friend in Israels Netanyahu despite genocide.

title: Russia-Ukraine war: List of key events, day 1,460
description: These are the key developments from day 1,460 of Russia’s war on Ukraine.

title: Tour Montparnasse is One of Paris’s Uglier Landmarks. It’s Getting a Makeover.
description: The Tour Montparnasse is one of Paris’s least-loved landmarks. After half a century, it’s finally being remodeled.
